canonical_question: How do I implement SSO with SAML and OpenID Connect?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: SAML 2.0 finalized 2005-03; OpenID Connect 1.0 finalized 2014-02; OIDC Federation 1.0 draft 2024

constraints: Always validate SAML assertion XML signatures server-side — never accept unsigned or client-validated assertions
OIDC nonce parameter must be validated on every authentication response to prevent replay attacks
Always validate issuer (iss) and audience (aud) claims in both SAML assertions and OIDC ID tokens
SAML assertions must be consumed within NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter time window with clock skew tolerance
Never store SAML assertions or OIDC tokens in localStorage — use httpOnly secure cookies or server-side sessions
SAML XML parsing must use defenses against XML Signature Wrapping (XSW) and XXE attacks

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need OAuth 2.0 API authorization without user identity (machine-to-machine)
Building a custom identity provider from scratch
Only need social login (Google, GitHub, Facebook) without enterprise SSO

often_confused_with.label: OAuth 2.0 (authorization, not authentication — OIDC adds the identity layer)

inputs_needed.question: Which SSO protocol do you need to implement?
What role does your application play?
Which identity provider will you integrate with?
